chunks.doc_name: Audit Approach | Medical Device Single Audit Program (MDSAP)
chunks.text: **Automatic language translation** Our website uses an automatic service to translate our content into different languages. These translations should be used as a guide only. ## Breadcrumb 1. [Home](/) 2. [Auditing Organizations](/auditing-organizations) 3. Audit Approach # Audit Approach ## Sidebar navigation ### [Auditing Organizations](/auditing-organizations) * [Audit Approach](/auditing-organizations/audit-approach) * [AO Assessment Program](/auditing-organizations/ao-assessment-program) * [Becoming Recognized](/auditing-organizations/becoming-recognized) * [AO Contact Details](/contact/ao-contact-details) * [AO Contributions](/auditing-organizations/ao-contributions) * [Feedback and complaints](/contact/feedback-and-complaints) A quality management system (QMS) is a formalized system that documents and defines processes, procedures and responsibilities to achieve quality policies, quality objectives and regulatory obligations. A QMS audit is a systematic, independent and objective evaluation of an organization's QMS. Third party certification bodies perform QMS audits of medical device company’s QMS to verify that organizational, regulatory and international standards requirements have been met. These bodies are called Auditing Organizations (or AOs) within the MDSAP. The MDSAP allows [competent auditors](https://www.imdrf.org/documents/competence-and-training-requirements-auditing-organizations) from a [recognized MDSAP AO](/contact/ao-contact-details "AO contact details") to conduct a single audit of a medical device organization's QMS. This single audit satisfies the QMS requirements of RAs participating in MDSAP including: * ISO13485:2016 - Medical devices – Quality management systems – Requirements for regulatory purposes and the regulatory requirements from relevant MDSAP RAs (see [ISO - ISO 13485 — Medical devices](https://www.iso.org/iso-13485-medical-devices.html)) * the Australian Therapeutic Goods (Medical Devices) Regulations (2002) Schedule 3 Part 1, excluding 1.6, and Schedule 3 Part 4 * the Brazilian Good Manufacturing Practices (RDC ANVISA 16/2013) * the Canadian Medical Devices Regulations (Parts 1 and 1.1) * the Japanese QMS ordinance (MHLW MO 169) * the American Quality System Regulation (21 CFR Part 820), and * other specific requirements of medical device regulatory authorities participating in the MDSAP program including 21 CFR Part 803 and 21 CFR Part 806. The MDSAP[Audit Approach](/documents/library/audit-approach "Audit Approach")was developed to ensure AOs performed QMS audits in a consistent way. It defines the audit sequence, instructions for auditing eachspecific process, and identifies the linkages between processes. The audit approach is based on a foundation of risk management principles. Further information on the MDSAP QMS is available in the[MDSAP QMS P0001: Quality System Manual](/documents/library/quality-system-manual "Quality System Manual")and the[MDSAP QMS F0001.1 QMS Policy and Objectives](/documents/library/qms-policy-and-objectives "QMS Policy and Objectives"). The audit sequence follows four primary processes: * Management process * Measurement, Analysis and Improvement process * Design and Development process * Production and Service Controls process. These primary processes all have linkages to the supporting Purchasing process, including supplier control and verification of purchased product. There are two additional supporting processes that are necessary to meet specific jurisdictional regulatory requirements: * Device Marketing Authorization and Facility Registration * Medical Device Adverse Events and Advisory Notice Reporting. Each process includes several tasks to be audited. While the sequence of processes to be audited is prescribed, auditors may audit tasks within a given process in any sequence to allow for an efficient and effective audit. The tasks required to be audited vary depending on if the audit is [an initial certification, surveillance, re-certification, or special audit](/manufacturers/how-be-mdsap-audit-ready/what-should-i-expect-initial-audit "What should I expect at an initial audit"). Audits may be conducted offsite in a

chunks.doc_name: Audit Approach | Medical Device Single Audit Program (MDSAP)
chunks.text: the sequence of processes to be audited is prescribed, auditors may audit tasks within a given process in any sequence to allow for an efficient and effective audit. The tasks required to be audited vary depending on if the audit is [an initial certification, surveillance, re-certification, or special audit](/manufacturers/how-be-mdsap-audit-ready/what-should-i-expect-initial-audit "What should I expect at an initial audit"). Audits may be conducted offsite in a documentation review format, onsite, or in [a remote capacity](/documents/library/remote-hybrid-auditing-program "Remote / Hybrid Auditing Program")or a combination of these, depending on the type and nature of the audit. For each audit conducted, AOs are required to determine [the duration of an audit](/documents/library/audit-time-determination-procedure "Audit Time Determination Procedure"), including how long auditors, or a team of auditors should spend auditing each process and task. [A tool](/documents/library/audit-duration-calculation-form-audit-model-2017 "Audit Duration Calculation Form (Audit Model 2017)")is available to assist with these calculations. After the audit has been conducted, the AO issues any nonconformities using a [standard template](/documents/library/nc-grading-and-exchange-form "NC Grading and Exchange Form ")and a [final report](/documents/library/medical-device-regulatory-audit-report "Medical Device Regulatory Audit Report") to the medical device manufacturer. If [nonconformities](/manufacturers/how-be-mdsap-audit-ready/what-should-i-expect-initial-audit "What should I expect at an initial audit") were raised, the manufacturer has the opportunity to formally respond and address these. After the successful closure of an initial or re-certification audit, the AO will issue an [MDSAP certificate](/documents/library/surveillance-audit-confirmation-notification-process "Surveillance Audit Confirmation Notification Process"). This certificate is an attestation by the AO that the facilities listed in the certificate have been audited against the listed criteria for the listed scope and found to conform to those requirements, including the regulatory requirements for the specific RAs. Certificates are not normally issued after surveillance audits. Within a [specified time period](/documents/library/post-audit-activities-and-timeline-policy "Post Audit Activities and Timeline Policy") after the audit, AOs are required to submit the audit report and nonconformities into the [Regulatory Exchange Platform-secure (REPs)](/documents/library/reps-user-account-management "REPs User Account Management"). REPs is an electronic repository where RAs can access information to support their pre- and post-market regulatory activities. Further detail regarding the MDSAP audit approach can be found in the [MDSAP Audit Procedures and Forms](/documents/audit-procedures-and-forms "Audit procedures and forms") and under [Manufacturers](/manufacturers/how-be-mdsap-audit-ready "How to be MDSAP audit ready"). For a list of recognized MDSAP AOs, click [here](/contact/ao-contact-details "AO contact details").
